Politics.headline: US Supreme Court rejects Colorado ban on LGBTQ child 'conversion therapy'
Politics.detail: In an eight-to-one decision, the high court ruled against a law banning the discredited practice on free speech grounds.

Politics.headline: Stephen Lewis, Canadian politician, diplomat and journalist, dead at 88
Politics.detail: Stephen Lewis, a giant in Canadian activism, is being remembered as a tireless fighter who demanded better of all.

Politics.headline: China and Pakistan present new Iran deal: Ceasefire for opening Hormuz
Politics.detail: China and Pakistan have presented a new deal regarding Iran, proposing a ceasefire in exchange for opening the Strait of Hormuz.

Politics.headline: Supreme Court weighs racism claim over jury selection in death penalty case
Politics.detail: The Supreme Court again considered alleged bias accusations against an infamous Mississippi prosecutor.

Finance & Economy.headline: Government notifies Finance Act 2026 that changes tax provisions from April 1
Finance & Economy.detail: The government has officially announced the Finance Act 2026, which includes changes to tax regulations taking effect from today, April 1st.

Finance & Economy.headline: Two-thirds of UK hospitality businesses plan to cut jobs and one in seven will close, survey finds
Finance & Economy.detail: A recent survey reveals that a significant portion of UK hospitality businesses are considering job cuts, with a concerning number potentially facing closure.

Finance & Economy.headline: The Dow surges more than 1,100 points as Wall Street soars to its best day since last spring
Finance & Economy.detail: Driven by renewed optimism, Wall Street experienced a significant surge, marking its best performance since last spring, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average climbing over 1,100 points.

Finance & Economy.headline: New I-T law, higher securities transaction tax on F&O trade to kick in from April 1
Finance & Economy.detail: Starting today, April 1st, a new Income Tax law and an increased securities transaction tax on Futures and Options (F&O) trading are being implemented.

Finance & Economy.headline: Iran war wipes $120bn off Dubai, Abu Dhabi stock markets
Finance & Economy.detail: Since the beginning of the US-Israel war on Iran, the stock markets in Dubai and Abu Dhabi have seen a loss of approximately $120 billion in value, making them among the most affected financial markets globally.

Technology.headline: Gmail celebrates 22 years by finally letting users change their addresses
Technology.detail: Google is celebrating Gmail's 22nd anniversary by allowing users to change their addresses, so users can update from old or unprofessional addresses.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: AI is becoming a force multiplier for cybercriminals, helping threat actors automate phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance while accelerating their attacks.

Technology.headline: Amazon Leo to Test Custom Satellite Dish With Israeli Firm
Technology.detail: Amazon's Project Leo will test a custom satellite dish with Israeli firm Get SAT, which sells satellite antenna equipment to clients, including defense groups and enterprises.

Technology.headline: Now's Your Chance: Google Expands Option to Change Your Gmail Address
Technology.detail: Google is expanding the option to change your Gmail address, allowing users to have their old and new Gmail addresses live side by side in their inbox, provided the name they want is available.

Technology.headline: Seattle VR gaming studio Polyarc announces 'significant' layoffs
Technology.detail: Seattle-based VR gaming studio Polyarc has announced 'significant' layoffs, impacting an undisclosed number of employees.

Science.headline: Pronatalists want more babies. Their solutions aren't rooted in science.
Science.detail: An analysis of pronatalist policies reveals that many of the proposed solutions for declining birth rates are not based on scientific evidence and may even be harmful.

Science.headline: A comet may have flipped its spin and entered into a death spiral.
Science.detail: Observations suggest a comet has undergone a dramatic change in its rotation and is now on a collision course with the sun.

Science.headline: Secrets of the Bees zooms in on life in a hive.
Science.detail: A new documentary provides an intimate look at the complex social lives and behaviors of bees within a hive.

Science.headline: SimCells successfully target and kill drug-resistant bacteria.
Science.detail: New research demonstrates that SimCells, a type of synthetic cell, can effectively target and eliminate drug-resistant bacteria in lab settings.

Science.headline: Lost mosaic reveals first image of female beast-fighter from the Roman era.
Science.detail: The discovery of a mosaic unveils the first known depiction of a female beast-fighter from the Roman period, providing new insights into the roles of women in ancient Roman society.

Health.headline: Parkinson's patients report better balance with Brazilian capoeira dance
Health.detail: Capoeira, a form of martial art disguised as dance, is now being used in Brazil to help patients with Parkinson's disease, and the patients say the specific movements of this once-outlawed exercise are improving their physical health.

Health.headline: FDA Issues Alert on Liver Injuries Linked to Vasculitis Drug
Health.detail: The FDA has issued an alert regarding liver injuries that are potentially linked to a drug used for vasculitis.

Health.headline: Weight Loss May Cut Cancer Risk in Adults With Obesity
Health.detail: According to a recent study, weight loss may reduce cancer risk in adults with obesity.

Health.headline: Simple Therapies Beat Drugs for Knee Arthritis Pain Relief
Health.detail: A major analysis of nearly 10,000 patients shows that simple, non-drug treatments like knee braces, hydrotherapy, and exercise can significantly ease knee osteoarthritis symptoms.

Health.headline: Sleeping 11 minutes longer each night shows surprising health benefit, study finds
Health.detail: A recent study has found that sleeping just 11 minutes longer each night can lead to surprising health benefits.

Sports.headline: 4-time champion Italy misses 3rd straight World Cup with loss in Bosnia
Sports.detail: Italy failed to qualify for the World Cup for the third time in a row after losing to Bosnia.

Sports.headline: PH booters denied Asian Cup spot despite Obermair's debut goal
Sports.detail: Despite a debut goal from Obermair, the Philippine national football team was denied a spot in the Asian Cup.

Sports.headline: 7-foot-9 Olivier Rioux is transferring from Florida
Sports.detail: Olivier Rioux, the 7-foot-9 basketball player, is transferring from the University of Florida.

Sports.headline: Rain or Shine forges record margin with huge victory over Blackwater
Sports.detail: Rain or Shine achieved a record-breaking victory over Blackwater in a recent game.

Sports.headline: Tennis-Sinner beats Lehecka in rain-hit Miami final to capture 'Sunshine Double'
Sports.detail: Jannik Sinner defeated Jiri Lehecka in a rain-affected Miami Open final to win the 'Sunshine Double'.

Entertainment.headline: Salman Khan MOBBED by fans at President Cup event; concerned netizens question security following death threats - WATCH
Entertainment.detail: Bollywood superstar Salman Khan made an appearance at the President Cup 2026 event in Thane, where he was mobbed by fans, raising security concerns among netizens following recent death threats. The security team struggled to keep the crowd away from the actor.

Entertainment.headline: 'Ramayana': Ranbir Kapoor reveals he first REJECTED Lord Rama role; says film and becoming a father to Raha Kapoor was a 'turning point'
Entertainment.detail: Ranbir Kapoor initially rejected the role of Lord Rama in the upcoming film 'Ramayana,' but later accepted it, citing that becoming a father to his daughter Raha Kapoor was a turning point in his life. He shared that the coincidence of playing Lord Rama and becoming a father was needed for a turning point in his life.

Entertainment.headline: Sony and TCL Sign Definitive Agreements for Strategic Partnership in the Home Entertainment Field
Entertainment.detail: Sony and TCL have signed definitive agreements to establish a joint venture in the home entertainment sector, with TCL holding 51% and Sony 49% of the shares. The New Company will handle product development, design, manufacturing, sales, and customer service for Consumer TVs (BRAVIA), B2B Flat Panel Displays (B2B BRAVIA), B2B LED Displays, projectors, and home audio equipment.

Entertainment.headline: US Army begins inquiry into helicopter fly-by of Kid Rock's Nashville home
Entertainment.detail: The U.S. Army has launched an inquiry into a recent incident involving two helicopters flying near Kid Rock's Nashville home. Kid Rock posted a video on social media showing the helicopters hovering close to his home as he stood beside a miniature Statue of Liberty and saluted the aircraft.

Entertainment.headline: Celine Dion Announces 1st Concert After Stiff-Person Syndrome Diagnosis
Entertainment.detail: Celine Dion has announced her first concert after being diagnosed with Stiff-Person Syndrome. The concert marks her return to the stage after nearly four years since announcing her diagnosis in 2022.

Crime & Justice.headline: Sides make closing arguments in the trial over the 2024 killing of a New York City police officer
Crime & Justice.detail: Prosecutors and defense attorneys have made their closing arguments in the trial of a man charged in the 2024 shooting death of a New York City police officer.

Crime & Justice.headline: Israel's parliament approves the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis.
Crime & Justice.detail: The article discusses the approval of the death penalty in Israel for Palestinians convicted of murdering Israelis and also mentions protests in the West Bank against the law.

Crime & Justice.headline: Slain college student's mother vows 'fight for justice' after illegal immigrant charged in Chicago killing
Crime & Justice.detail: Sheridan Gorman, 18, was allegedly shot and killed near Loyola University Chicago by Jose Medina-Medina, an illegal immigrant from Venezuela, according to authorities.

Crime & Justice.headline: Matt Hancock 'assaulted on London Underground' as man arrested.
Crime & Justice.detail: The article reports on an assault against Matt Hancock on the London Underground, resulting in an arrest.

Crime & Justice.headline: Expansion of HMP Parc in Wales should be paused, MPs say
Crime & Justice.detail: This article discusses the concerns raised by MPs regarding the expansion of HMP Parc in Wales and the call to pause the project.

Education.headline: Georgia could become the first state to require weapon detection in all public schools
Education.detail: If passed, this legislation would mandate weapon detection systems in every public school across the state, aiming to enhance student safety.

Education.headline: North Carolina lowered its principal attrition rate. Those who stay report working conditions haven't changed.
Education.detail: While principal attrition has decreased in North Carolina, a recent report indicates that the working conditions for principals who remained in their positions have not seen significant improvements.

Education.detail: The Education Department is taking steps to transfer responsibilities related to student loans and the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) to the Treasury Department.

Education.headline: U.S. Department of Education's Office for Civil Rights Issues Letter of Impending Enforcement to San Jose State University on Title IX Compliance.

Education.headline: Westlake High athletes formed 'Make Great Plays' to help elementary school students believe
Education.detail: 'Make Great Plays' is a group of Westlake High athletes who give elementary school students a chance to dream, excel and believe they can achieve anything.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Hegseth targets Black, female officers more qualified than him
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial criticizes Hegseth for allegedly targeting Black and female officers who are more qualified than himself.

World.detail: The UK will pay France an additional £16 million in a stopgap deal to enhance patrols on Channel beaches.

World.detail: US and Israeli forces have struck Iranian steel plants, while President Trump claims the war with Iran could end in two to three weeks.

World.detail: President Trump suggested that the UK and other nations should go to the Strait of Hormuz and "take" oil.

Zimbabwe.detail: A hit and run driver was arrested in Harare.
